deductions.reason: The tutor repeatedly used multi-part, checklist-heavy replies and long scaffolds when shorter interactive steps would have fit the student's repeated time-pressure better. The replies were structured and useful, but they often reduced brevity and immediate responsiveness.

grade.overview: Across the three transcripts, the tutor stayed strongly assignment-anchored, refused to provide submission-ready writing, and consistently pushed the student to generate the thesis, case analyses, and general principle themselves. It also gave solid method-level guidance by stress-testing the student's rule against Switch, Footbridge, Loop, Fuel, and Blood, and it generally built on the student's progress rather than restarting. The main weakness was communication efficiency: despite the student's repeated urgency, many responses remained longer and more checklist-heavy than necessary. I did not take an added deduction for dense philosophical language because that judgment was more ambiguous in this course context.
